lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Carla
lines.text: Oh, hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem upset... What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Carla
lines.text: My son fell ill with the plague...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's awful, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Carla
lines.text: It would be easier to cope with if I could have spent his last few days with him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why didn't you?

lines.speaker: Carla
lines.text: Those mourners came and whisked him away. He didn't even seem that ill, I thought it was a common cold... But the mourners said that he was infected and had to be taken away.

lines.speaker: Carla
lines.text: Two days later the mourners returned and told me he had died.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Again, I'm sorry. Life can be harsh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did the plague come from?

lines.speaker: Carla
lines.text: It's down to King Tyras. He and his men brought the plague here from the west, and then left us to suffer. One day he'll pay for what he's done!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have there been many victims of the plague?

lines.speaker: Carla
lines.text: You could say that... I've heard reports that half of West Ardougne is infected! Many have lost friends and family...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It sounds an awful way to live.

lines.speaker: Carla
lines.text: People are very depressed and scared. I've never met anyone fully infected though. I suppose we should be grateful to the mourners for that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hope things get easier for you.

lines.speaker: Carla
lines.text: Me too...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Carla, how are you doing?

lines.speaker: Carla
lines.text: I'm okay. The loss of my son is something that I'll never be over. At least no one else has to suffer at the hands of those mourners though.
